fields.summary: Create a cardone ventures employee checkbox

content.text: Please build a checkbox on the patient account record type called “Cardone Ventures Employee”. This field will need to push into ALL opportunity record types if the box is selected for us to be able to pull reporting. However, the master is on the account level.

requestType.name: General Help Desk Request
requestType.description: Submit a Request or report and issue; fields below are based upon your selection.

status.description: This was auto-generated by Jira Service Management during workflow import
status.name: Escalated

content.text: We have assigned your request HEAL-33054 to an appropriate Help Desk team member. They will reach out to you shortly with an update.
Ashlee and I were thinking out loud. The current existing accounts that are CV employees - with the “CV Employee” check box would we have to go back retroactively to mark the check box?
The issue HEAL-33054 has been assigned to me. I’m taking a look now and will keep you updated as I make progress.
Let me know if there’s any additional context that might help.

Cardone Ventures employee Accounts will need to be manually identified and the Account checkbox marked for each one. Existing Opportunity records will automatically reflect the correct value once the Account is updated, so no Opportunity-level backfill is required.
Once the fields are in Prod, how can I identify which Accounts are Cardone Ventures employee Accounts?
Added `Cardone_Ventures_Employee__c` to `Account-Account Layout` in the Account Summary section, after `Do_Not_Sync_to_Biocanic__c`.
PersonAccount-Medical Team Page Layout` — after Brand__c
`PersonAccount-New Sales Account Page Layout` — after VIP_Account__c
`PersonAccount-Retention Patient Account Page Layout` — after Brand__c
`PersonAccount-Retention Patient PersAcct Page Layout` — after Brand__c
Added `Cardone_Ventures_Employee__c` (Readonly) to all 31 in-use Opportunity page layouts, placed immediately after `AccountId` in the "Opportunity Information" section on every layout.
